name: Remember in Sahara
summary: Capture dictated text, selected text, URLs, share-sheet input, or clipboard text into Sahara memory.

steps.name: Load paired endpoint and token
steps.notes: Values come from `sahara mobile pair --json`. Store endpoint and token inside the Shortcut or an iCloud Keychain-backed secure note.

steps.name: Read share input
steps.notes: Accept text and URLs from the iOS share sheet. Do not inspect app-specific data beyond what iOS passes explicitly.

steps.name: Fallback to clipboard
steps.notes: Used for WhatsApp or apps that do not pass selected text cleanly. The user must copy the message first.

steps.name: Ask for dictation when empty
steps.notes: Allows the Siri phrase to capture spoken knowledge when there is no share input or clipboard text.

steps.name: Ask for optional tags
steps.notes: Comma-separated tags are split and trimmed before sending.

steps.name: Ask for optional source note
steps.notes: Stored as source_id when present. This is user-authored provenance, not scraped app metadata.

steps.name: Generate idempotency key
steps.notes: Generate once before the POST. Reuse the same UUID for a retry of the same capture.

steps.name: POST capture JSON
steps.notes: POST to endpoint + /v1/memories with Authorization bearer token and JSON body.

steps.name: Show and speak status
steps.notes: Display saved, already_saved, or saved_index_pending. Speaking is limited to status, never full memory content.
